primary_object_card.object_statement: A finite shell-indexed field of possible futures compresses inward through compatibility into a present survivor, while the past-facing outward record preserves what survived.

source_anchors.quote_or_summary: Literal shells carry possible futures inward; the past-facing record moves outward.

primary_object_card.plain_language_definition: A finite field of literal shell-indexed possible futures around event_x. Future continuations on the shell Sigma_r compress inward through compatibility weights into a present survivor, while the past-facing outward_record preserves what survived. Entropy, Axis0, FEP, nested PEPS2D/Hopfield or other certified carrier readouts, and QIT cuts are readouts or carriers derived from this object, never replacements for it.

native_terms.jk fuzz: The finite set of admissible future possibilities or continuations carried on the shell around event_x.
native_terms.present survivor: The compressed present state produced by compatibility-weighting the possible futures.
native_terms.shell: Sigma_r is the finite spherical shell or stratum at radius or support-order r around event_x.

weirdness_signature: future possibilities are represented as first-class shell contents
future compression flows inward while the past record flows outward
present survivor is derived from compatibility-weighted possible futures
allowed_operations: compute compatibility weights over future continuations
compress future continuations inward into a present survivor
derive entropy, QIT, and certified carrier/readout bundles only after compression
required_invariants: present survivor is derived from weighted futures, never asserted directly
forbidden_substitutions: CTMRG on structured 3D tensors as load-bearing carrier
negative_controls: replace compatibility weights with uniform labels
promote scalar entropy directly to Axis0
primary_object_card.domain: finite shell stack, admissible continuation set, finite carrier state, active constraints
primary_object_card.codomain_or_output: present survivor state, outward record, and derived readout bundle
primary_object_card.adapter_policy: Adapters may expose the object to QIT, nested PEPS2D/Hopfield, ITensors-MPS, exact dense, QuantumClifford, FEP, or graph tools only after naming what they preserve and lose; adapter output can never become the object.
primary_object_card.artifact_surface: Hermes wizard harness JSON packet plus repo-guard validation output recorded under wiki/wizard/hermes-version-current/packets/

lateral_mappings.label: Nested PEPS2D / Hopfield connection geometry carrier
lateral_mappings.target: finite spinor/tensor carrier where Hopfield/PEPS2D bonds form the connection surface — L/R Weyl spinors on nested Hopf-torus sheets, geometry read from plaquette holonomy, Laplacian spectrum, heat trace, curvature, and terrain/operator signatures
loses: literal global shell unless shell metadata is carried
preconditions: adapter output cannot become the object
lateral_mappings.kill_control: drop shell metadata; any Axis0 or gravity-model claim must then fail; single-fused PEPS3D contraction is retired — use nested PEPS2D layers instead

lateral_mappings.label: Hermes three-council proxy readout
lateral_mappings.target: council-stage progress signal for the object preflight
preserves: a single pass/fail preflight signal before councils run
preconditions: object card already validated by the repo guard
lateral_mappings.kill_control: if councils claim object progress without a passing card, the proxy is void

lateral_mappings.label: FEP update
preconditions: must be derived as a local expression after the object is instantiated
lateral_mappings.kill_control: if an FEP update exists without a possible-future set, it cannot claim object preservation

extended_constraints: nested PEPS2D / Hopfield connection geometry / ITensors-MPS / exact dense / QuantumClifford carrier with error certificate

fixation_breakers: demote a repeated target to proxy unless the primary object fields are preserved

loop_contract.repair_rule: repair the first object-preservation error before adding new exploration lanes
loop_contract.stop_condition: all object-preservation errors pass, or a named primary object field is impossible to instantiate

method_contracts.question_is_not_authorization: A question or research prompt is not implementation authorization; implementation requires an explicit bounded task, artifact surface, and verification gate.
method_contracts.order_check_separate: Order must be checked separately from content similarity; a sequence match is not admitted unless the order-sensitive control also passes.
method_contracts.baseline_preservation: Preserve the visible baseline and current artifact ceiling while runtime or packet errors are debugged; do not upgrade claims during repair.
method_contracts.falsifier_first: For each target claim, name the strongest falsifier first, name the decisive check, and classify the result as killed, open, or survived.
method_contracts.feynman_testability: Each claim must name the concrete operation, observable, and pass/fail condition before it can be treated as testable.
method_contracts.synthesis_refusal: Refuse to merge surviving alternatives when receipts do not exclude them; name the split instead of smoothing it away.
method_contracts.human_offload: Resolve all tool-retrievable or controller-resolvable work before asking the human; ask only when a real choice, permission, or unavailable input remains.

evidence_spine.wizard_runtime: Hermes session runs the repo Wizard v4.3 object-preservation guard (scripts/wizard_v4_3_object_preservation.py) as a preflight over the Wizard v4.2 Max-Assembly council runtime; v4.3 does not replace the v4.2 council runtime and Hermes does not fork the validator.
evidence_spine.claim_ceiling: object-preservation preflight only; no sim, physics, Axis0, flux, or formal admission claim
blocked_consumers: v4.2 council progress claim without a passing object card
sim/proof promotion without source lock and audit-spine receipts

source_math_locks.preserves: primary object card before councils, sims, or follow-up loops claim object progress
source_math_locks.status: repo authority surface (sole authority for v4.3)

claude_pattern_cards.authority_reason: Claude material is reference only, not authority; the repo spec and this validator are the authority. Hermes ports the pattern as a skill but gates through the repo guard.
